Twelve bears eat five pounds of honey in one day. At the same rate, how many pounds of honey will 20 bears eat in three days?

}

\textbf{Answer:} Assume that a bear eats $h$ pounds of honey in a given day. Then 12 bears will eat $12h$ pounds of honey in a day. Therefore
\[5=12h\]
so $h=\frac5{12}.$ In three days, 20 bears eat 60 days worth of honey. Therefore 20 bears eat
\[60h=60\cdot\frac5{12}=\boxed{25}\]
pounds of honey in three days.

Many students may find it easier to make a table, like this:\\

\begin{tabular}{c|c|c}
Bears & Days & Pounds of Honey\\
\hline
12&5 & 1\\
12&3 &15\\
4&3&5\\
20&3&25\\
\end{tabular}\\
\\
Using either method, we have $\boxed{25}$ days.
